title: multiple right joins produce wrong result
description:
### What happens?

I perform the following multiple right join involving subquery, it produces wrong results.

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 DOUBLE);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 DOUBLE);
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (0.0);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES ('-0.0');
INSERT INTO t2(c0) VALUES (0.0);


SELECT subQuery1.s1, t2.c0 FROM
(
  SELECT t0.c0 as s1, t1.c0 as s2
  FROM t0
        RIGHT JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 = t1.c0 WHERE NOT t0.c0
) as subQuery1
RIGHT JOIN t2 on CAST(subQuery1.s1 AS TEXT) = CAST(t2.c0 AS TEXT);
---  NULL   │  0.0  , Wrong result
--- Expected Result:  0.0  │  0.0 
```

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 DOUBLE);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 DOUBLE);
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (0.0);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES ('-0.0');
INSERT INTO t2(c0) VALUES (0.0);


SELECT subQuery1.s1, t2.c0 FROM
(
  SELECT t0.c0 as s1, t1.c0 as s2
  FROM t0
        RIGHT JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 = t1.c0 WHERE NOT t0.c0
) as subQuery1
RIGHT JOIN t2 on CAST(subQuery1.s1 AS TEXT) = CAST(t2.c0 AS TEXT);
---  NULL   │  0.0  , Wrong result
--- Expected Result:  0.0  │  0.0 
```

MacOS

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3(c0 REAL, c1 TEXT);
CREATE TABLE t4(c0 REAL, c1 TEXT);
INSERT INTO t4(c0, c1) VALUES (0.0, 'negative zero');

SELECT subQuery2.s1, t4.c0 FROM (SELECT t3.c0 as s1, t4.c0 as s2 FROM t3 LEFT JOIN t4 ON t3.c0 = t4.c0 WHERE t3.c0 IS NULL) as subQuery2 LEFT JOIN t4 ON CAST(subQuery2.s1 AS TEXT) = CAST(t4.c0 AS TEXT);
key_bug_features: multiple right joins with subquery produce incorrect results
